name: Real-Time Messaging with Pub/Sub
description: Implement real-time event broadcasting using Redis publish/subscribe channels for decoupled microservice communication.
prerequisites: Two terminal windows for publisher and subscriber

steps.title: Start a subscriber on a channel
steps.description: Open a terminal and subscribe to a named channel to listen for messages.
steps.tip: The subscriber blocks and waits for messages. You will need a second terminal for the publisher.

steps.title: Publish a message to the channel
steps.description: In a second terminal, send a message to all subscribers on the channel.

steps.title: Subscribe with pattern matching
steps.description: Use PSUBSCRIBE to listen to multiple channels matching a glob pattern.
steps.tip: This matches events.user, events.order, events.payment, etc.

steps.title: Publish to a patterned channel
steps.description: Send a message to a specific channel that pattern subscribers will receive.

steps.title: Check active channels and subscribers
steps.description: Inspect which pub/sub channels are currently active.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Redis Pub/Sub Demo
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Active channels before ==="
redis-cli PUBSUB CHANNELS '*'

echo ""
echo "=== Starting subscriber in background ==="
redis-cli SUBSCRIBE notifications &
SUB_PID=$!
sleep 1

echo ""
echo "=== Publishing messages ==="
redis-cli PUBLISH notifications '{"event":"user.signup","userId":1001}'
redis-cli PUBLISH notifications '{"event":"user.login","userId":1002}'
redis-cli PUBLISH notifications '{"event":"order.created","orderId":5001}'

sleep 1
kill $SUB_PID 2>/dev/null || true

echo ""
echo "=== Active channels after ==="
redis-cli PUBSUB CHANNELS '*'
echo "=== Number of subscribers ==="
redis-cli PUBSUB NUMSUB notifications

faq.question: Are pub/sub messages persisted?
faq.answer: No. Redis pub/sub is fire-and-forget. If no subscriber is listening when a message is published, it is lost. Use Redis Streams if you need message persistence and consumer groups.

faq.question: Can pub/sub work across Redis Cluster nodes?
faq.answer: Yes, published messages are broadcast to all cluster nodes so subscribers on any node receive them. However, this adds network overhead in large clusters.
